schema_text:
Dialect: mysql
Tables:
  students: [student_id (INT), first_name (VARCHAR(100)), last_name (VARCHAR(100)), enrollment_date (DATE), gpa (DECIMAL(3,2)), major (VARCHAR(100))] PK=student_id
  courses: [course_id (INT), course_name (VARCHAR(200)), credits (INT), instructor (VARCHAR(200))] PK=course_id
  enrollments: [enrollment_id (INT), student_id (INT), course_id (INT), grade (VARCHAR(5)), semester (VARCHAR(20))] PK=enrollment_id
Foreign Keys:
  enrollments.student_id -> students.student_id
  enrollments.course_id -> courses.course_id
question: What are the enrollment id, student id, course id, grade, semester of enrollments where grade is 'example'?

schema_text:
Dialect: sqlite
Tables:
  products: [product_id (INTEGER), name (TEXT), category (TEXT), price (REAL), stock_quantity (INTEGER), supplier (TEXT)] PK=product_id
  sales: [sale_id (INTEGER), product_id (INTEGER), sale_date (TEXT), quantity_sold (INTEGER), revenue (REAL)] PK=sale_id
Foreign Keys:
  sales.product_id -> products.product_id
question: Retrieve the top 5 products by lowest product id
sql: SELECT "product_id", "name", "category", "price", "stock_quantity", "supplier" FROM "products" ORDER BY "product_id" ASC LIMIT 5

schema_text:
Dialect: postgres
Tables:
  customers: [customer_id (SERIAL), name (VARCHAR(200)), email (VARCHAR(255)), city (VARCHAR(100)), country (VARCHAR(100)), created_at (TIMESTAMP)] PK=customer_id
  orders: [order_id (SERIAL), customer_id (INT), order_date (DATE), total_amount (NUMERIC(10,2)), status (VARCHAR(50))] PK=order_id
  order_items: [item_id (SERIAL), order_id (INT), product_name (VARCHAR(200)), quantity (INT), unit_price (NUMERIC(8,2))] PK=item_id
Foreign Keys:
  orders.customer_id -> customers.customer_id
  order_items.order_id -> orders.order_id
question: List the first 5 customers by lowest customer id
sql: SELECT "customer_id", "name", "email", "city", "country", "created_at" FROM "customers" ORDER BY "customer_id" ASC LIMIT 5
